best_image_source: Arapahoe County Aerials aerial photo dated 03/01/2024
roof_system: Likely single-ply membrane roof with multiple curbs/penetrations and perimeter edge metal
roof_age_estimate: Unknown from imagery; appears moderate-age based on wear and weathering
observations: Large low-slope roof with generally uniform dark membrane coverage.
Visible widespread surface weathering/fading and light dirt accumulation across the field of roof.
No obvious large open tears, blow-offs, or widespread membrane failures visible from aerial view.
Multiple rooftop penetrations, vents, and curbs are present; flashing detail quality cannot be fully confirmed from this altitude.

Some minor discoloration and patch-like areas are visible near roof edges and around select penetrations, suggesting routine maintenance history or localized repairs possible unless proven otherwise on-site.
summary: The roof appears to be a large low-slope commercial membrane system in generally serviceable condition with moderate weathering. Aerial imagery does not show major catastrophic damage or significant ponding, but the roof does show age-related surface wear and numerous penetrations that warrant periodic inspection.
recommendation: Continue routine maintenance and schedule a close-up roof inspection to verify flashing, sealant condition, and any localized repair areas around penetrations and perimeter transitions. No immediate full-roof replacement is indicated from the aerial image alone.
